behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser validation/formatting logic in a single local component; no module handoff is required to trigger it (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are absent. Rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically about parser rejection/error messaging, which is local function/object behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser directly with representative invalid cron strings and assert structured error type plus message content pinpoints the invalid field/syntax.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including last-day-of-month and nearest-weekday semantics.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly occur at parser->schedule-object->calculator semantic handoff for special cron modifiers, not just inside one pure function (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness is deterministic (so not System/Agentic). Rejected System because behavior text only requires timestamp correctness for cron semantics, not environment-dependent infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Parse cron expressions containing standard and edge modifiers, feed them through the real scheduling pipeline from start time, and assert the next N timestamps match expected fixtures for each case.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules that fire within a configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: The failure is at interaction between schedule generation and overlap-gap policy/reporting modules; unit-testing overlap math alone can miss boundary mismatches (MOCK-MISS). No required real OS/network/subprocess and no model non-determinism. Rejected System because behavior text is local schedule conflict logic, not environment behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create two real schedule objects, generate occurrences over a window, run overlap detection with a concrete minimum-gap config, and assert both detection outcome and reported conflict intervals.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: Needs real interaction between scheduler logic and timezone/DST conversion library with actual zone rules; many failures are in that wiring and rule interpretation. It does not require subprocesses/network/OS events by behavior text, and remains deterministic for fixed tzdata. Rejected System because text specifies DST-correct run-time computation, not runtime OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real timezone database in-process, run schedule computation around known DST transition dates for a target zone, and assert expected handling for nonexistent (spring-forward) and ambiguous (fall-back) 2:30 AM local times.

behaviors.description: If computed next run is already in the past, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is policy engine + scheduling state/time computation interaction; unit-testing only policy branching can miss real handoff/state interpretation bugs (MOCK-MISS). No required real infrastructure or AI output. Rejected System because behavior text focuses on policy decisioning from computed times, which is testable in-process with controlled clock inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scheduler computation with missed-run policy config and controlled current time; simulate downtime gaps and assert resulting action (skip/backfill) and resulting scheduled execution window/state updates.
